hypothesis: Universal machine learning interatomic potentials trained on general inorganic and organic datasets can accurately predict structural, mechanical, and thermodynamic properties of metal-organic frameworks without system-specific fine-tuning, with performance varying systematically across model architectures and training datasets such that models incorporating larger, more diverse training sets and equivariant architectures will achieve lower structural deviations, higher MD stability rates, and more accurate bulk moduli and heat capacities compared to DFT reference calculations.
